note: Full Manderly-furnished wedding feast menu: cod cakes, winter squash, hills of neeps, wheels of cheese, smoking mutton slabs, charred black beef ribs, three great pies (wagon-wheel width, flaky crust, carrots/onions/turnips/parsnips/mushrooms/"seasoned pork" in savory brown gravy) — the "pork" pies are the frey-pies; the most loaded food-description block in the arc

Wines at Manderly feast: black stout, yellow beer, wines red/gold/purple from the warm south "brought up from the warm south on fat-bottomed ships and aged in his deep cellars"; Arbor gold specified as the pie pairing
Merman's Court welcome feast (Wylis's return): "lamprey pie and venison with roasted chestnuts" — named dishes, Wyman describes them to Davos during the secret meeting

Wyman eats six portions, two from each of the three pies — "smacking his lips and slapping his belly and stuffing himself until the front of his tunic was half-brown with gravy stains and his beard was flecked with crumbs of crust" — performed gluttony-as-cover; load-bearing behavioral description

Wyman's feast-performance: "the very picture of the jolly fat man, laughing and smiling, japing with the other lords and slapping them on the back, calling out to the musicians for this tune or that tune" — physical/behavioral description; fake joy as mask
Wyman self-description: "I am too fat to sit a horse … My body has become a prison more dire than the Wolf's Den" — load-bearing self-description; belongs in wyman-manderly ## Quotes

Roose food-tasting every dish before eating: "No dish so much as touches Roose's lips until he sees Lord Wyman eat of it first" — Roose suspects poison but not cannibalism; Dustin notes this; Roose's miscalibrated threat model is itself load-bearing dramatic irony

Lady Dustin on Wyman: "Have you ever seen a fat man so happy? He is almost dancing. Serving with his own hands." — Dustin's ironic misread of Wyman; paired with her later "the fat man would like to kill us all … he does not have the belly for it" dismissal

Wyman calls for "The Night That Ended" and "brave young Danny Flint" — both songs carry dark thematic weight (murder at the Wall); Abel/Mance as singer in Winterfell is a separate spy-arc thread; Abel slug = mance-rayder
supper before the Blue Bard arrest: "ham studded with cloves and basted with honey and dried cherries" + "baked apples with a sharp white cheese" (castle sow butchered, no wild boar on hand)

supper with Falyse & Balman: "hippocras and buttered beets, hot-baked bread, herb-crusted pike, and ribs of wild boar. Cersei had become very fond of boar since Robert's death" (grim irony — Robert killed by a boar)
supper with Ser Kevan: "a simple supper of beets and bread and bloody beef with a flagon of Dornish red" — Kevan "scarce touched his wine cup"
failed wedding-morning breakfast: "two boiled eggs, a loaf of bread, and a pot of honey" — bloody half-formed chick in first egg; "Take this away and bring me hot spiced wine"
grim prison register: "a bowl of some waterly grey gruel" (flung at the septa), then "bread and fish and demanded wine to wash it down. No wine appeared" — Cersei's abasement through food
Tommen's wedding feast: Cersei "drank several cups of wine and pushed her food around a golden plate" / "The wine tastes of bile"
Cersei's strongwine register: "she poured herself a cup of strongwine and drank it by the window, watching the shadows lengthen across the yard"

first description of the High Sparrow: grey-brown closely-trimmed beard, hair in a hard knot, frayed patched robes, sleeves rolled scrubbing floors, "His feet are bare... hideous as well, hard and horny things, thick with callus"
the sparrows encamped on Baelor's plaza: "Their cookfires filled the air with smoke and stinks. Roughspun tents and miserable hovels made of mud and scrap wood besmirched the pristine white marble"
Baelor's marble statue "waist-deep in a heap of bones and skulls... A crow sat atop one such, enjoying a dry, leathery feast. Flies were everywhere" — war-dead brought to the Sept
Maggy the Frog's appearance (flashback): "short, squat, and warty, with pebbly greenish jowls. Her teeth were gone and her dugs hung down to her knees" — candidate Quotes/Appearance on maggy-the-frog

Maggy's tent interior (flashback): cinnamon, nutmeg, pepper, almond milk, cloves, lemongrass, saffron; "a dim green light" from an iron basilisk-head brazier "that made the walls of the tent look cold and dead and rotten"

the Blue Bard's appearance: "supple blue calfskin" boots, "fine blue wool" breeches, "pale blue silk slashed with shiny blue satin" tunic, hair "dye[d] blue, in the Tyroshi fashion... washed in rosewater" — candidate Appearance on blue-bard
Margaery's sept cell: "eight feet long and six feet wide, no furnishings but a straw-stuffed pallet and a bench for prayer, a ewer of water, a copy of The Seven-Pointed Star, and a candle"
the crown's debt to the Faith named exactly: "Nine hundred thousand six hundred and seventy-four dragons" — forgiven in Cersei's rearming bargain (overlay candidate for cersei-rearms-the-faith node)

the crystal-and-spun-gold crown Tywin gave the old High Septon, now SOLD by the High Sparrow ("That crown has been sold. So have the others in our vaults, and all our rings") — the Faith's ostentatious-poverty

Cersei's hubris immediately before her capture: "The younger queen whose coming she'd foretold was finished, and if that prophecy could fail, so could the rest. No golden shrouds, no valonqar, I am free of your croaking malice at last" — overlay candidate for maggy-the-frogs-prophecy node

Cersei's nightmare: Tyrion's "thick hands, and strong" coil around her neck, "she was making the same sound her son had made, the terrible thin sucking sound that marked Joff's last breath" — valonqar/Joffrey echo
first AFFC valonqar beat: "It is blood I need, not water. Tyrion's blood, the blood of the valonqar... his fingers had closed around her neck, and she could feel them beginning to tighten"
Cersei contemplating killing the High Sparrow "just as I did the other" — ironic foreshadowing of her own downfall by the Faith she rearms

Qyburn torturing the Blue Bard: "a wet red eye wept blood... Part of her wanted to close her eyes, to turn away, to make it stop. But she was the queen and this was treason. Lord Tywin would not have turned away" — Cersei-voice candidate

Quiet Isle supper, the best meal Brienne ever ate: "loaves of crusty bread still warm from the ovens, crocks of fresh-churned butter, honey from the septry's hives, and a thick stew of crabs, mussels, and at least three different kinds of fish" + mead + sweet cider

Septon Meribald's donkey relief-food inventory for the broken/poor: "seeds and nuts and dried fruit, oaten porridge, flour, barley bread, three wheels of yellow cheese... salt cod... salt mutton for Dog... onions, carrots, turnips, two sacks of beans, four of barley, and nine of oranges"
crossroads-inn wartime subsistence: only horse meat to eat, the orphan children hungry
Meribald distributes his last oranges to the inn orphans ("his last till spring"); a boy who had never tasted an orange — hospitality/charity register
Brotherhood cave food, starvation register: cold greasy stew, hard bread, harder cheese; Brienne "never eaten anything half so good" (near-death context)
riverlands famine signal: "Between rains, floods, fire, and war, they had lost two harvests and a good part of a third. An early winter would mean famine all across the riverlands."
Septon Meribald's "broken men" sermon — load-bearing set-piece on war, desertion, and what breaks a smallfolk soldier; thematic PARALLELS candidate (connects to Sandor's arc)
Oathkeeper at candlelight (overlay candidate for oathkeeper node ## Quotes): "Black and red the ripples ran, deep within the steel. Valyrian steel, spell-forged."

Lady Stoneheart revealed: bronze circlet with iron swords; "dry brittle white hair," mottled green-grey brow with brown blooms of decay, flesh in ragged strips from eyes to jaw, some rips gaping to the skull — appearance overlay for catelyn-rises-as-lady-stoneheart
Gendry's Robert-Baratheon build (Brienne sees Renly at first, then revises): black hair, blue eyes, heavy shoulders, brawnier than Renly — the bastard-resemblance beat
the road of hanged men with heraldic badges and SALT packed in their mouths (reference to the Saltpans raid) — the Stoneheart vengeance campaign made visible; full sigil enumeration
"'Sapphires,' she whispered at him, as she gave her blade a hard twist that made him shudder." — Brienne to Rorge (callback to Jaime's sapphire gambit that saved her)
Elder Brother on the Saltpans raider: "I will not call them wolves. Wolves are nobler than that . . . and so are dogs, I think." — attach to sandor-clegane and/or elder-brother-quiet-isle

Long Jeyne Heddle: "Guest right don't mean so much as it used to . . . Not since m'lady come back from the wedding." — the Brotherhood-under-Stoneheart's collapse of guest right; echoes the Red Wedding

THE GRAVEDIGGER = Sandor-alive signal: a lame brother "bigger than Brienne" digging graves at the Quiet Isle, who scratches Dog's ear — near-canon that Sandor survives; load-bearing for the Hound/Sandor character unit
Rhaegar's rubies wash up at the Quiet Isle: "Six have been found. We are all waiting for the seventh." — long-standing series Chekhov's gun
Meribald's tale of the great she-wolf pack on the Trident "known to bring aurochs down all by herself" — almost certainly Nymeria's pack; Arya-arc foreshadowing

the most visceral on-page kingslaying account ("Those purple eyes grew huge then… A single slash across his throat was all it took to end it… A king should die harder than this") — the slaying-of-aerys node has NO vivid evidence quote; attach as ## Quotes
Aerys voice: "The traitors want my city… but I'll give them naught but ashes. Let Robert be king over charred bones and cooked meat." — load-bearing for aerys-ii-targaryen ## Quotes

Aerys's dragon-rebirth delusion: "Like Aerion Brightfire before him, Aerys thought the fire would transform him… reborn as a dragon" — Targaryen fire-mythology; resonates with Dany/dragon-birth + Aerion-Brightflame
Jaime identity-arc: "The hand that made me Kingslayer… Who am I now?" — attach to jaime-lannister ## Quotes

wildfire cache geography ("Beneath Baelor's Sept and the hovels of Flea Bottom, under stables and storehouses, at all seven gates, even in the cellars of the Red Keep itself") — textual anchor if a wildfire-cache-locations overlay is ever built; attach to wildfire-plot
Tywin's culpability admission: "Elia need not have been harmed at all, that was sheer folly. By herself she was nothing." — attach to murder-of-elia / tywin-lannister
Tywin on the rape ("even you will not accuse me of giving that command") + Rhaenys/Amory Lorch ("a soft silk pillow") — culpability-tier evidence for amory-lorch / murder-of-elia
"crimson Lannister cloaks" the children's bodies were wrapped in — the visual proof of Tywin's deliberate staging; now on tywin-presents-bodies node, but also a murder-of-elia ## Quotes candidate
Aegon's death detail ("torn from his mother's breast and dashed his head against a wall") — Ned's account; complements Gregor's confession on the murder node

White Book entry on Jaime ("slew King Aerys II at the foot of the Iron Throne. Thereafter known as the 'Kingslayer.' Pardoned for his crime by King Robert I Baratheon.") — canonical in-universe record; cite_ref for slaying-of-aerys node
Jaime's "what-if": briefly tempted to proclaim Viserys king with Tywin as Hand before sitting the throne — on-page deliberation; jaime-lannister character depth
Inn of the Kneeling Man meal: charred horse steaks, onions fried in bacon grease, stale oatcakes; Jaime+Cleos ale, Brienne cider; midnight camp = oatcakes + salt fish (war-ration grim register)
Roose Bolton's Harrenhal supper: cheese, bread, cold meat, fruit; red wine (Jaime), water (Brienne), hippocras (Bolton); Bolton's prunes + bloody roast — fastidious-cold-host hospitality contrast
Dragonpit wildfire-cache discovery (whore fell through a rotted floor, found the jars, drank one mistaking it for wine) — the recovered-Rossart-cache that seeds Blackwater; grim dark-comedy detail

Hizdahr's feast for the Yunkai'i: camel, crocodile, singing squid, lacquered ducks, spiny grubs, goat, ham, horse, dog four ways — "No Ghiscari feast was complete without a course of dog" (grim register; hospitality/politics)

Daznak royal box: figs, dates, melons, pomegranates, pecans, peppers + "a big bowl of honeyed locusts" (the poisoned bowl; Belwas eats it all) + peddlers' dog sausages, roast onions, unborn puppies on a stick (grim arena food)
Tattered Prince at the Purple Lotus: stale bread, unspeakable stew "Grease and salt... Dog, she says, but I think rat is more likely" (Meereen siege-poverty food)
Fall of Astapor starvation: survivors "ate cats and rats and leather. A horsehide was a feast"; King Cutthroat/Queen Whore accused of feasting on the slain (cannibalism, grim register)
Barristan's knighting-feast memory: "ribs of wild boar, prepared the Dornish way with dragon peppers, so hot they burned his mouth" (47-yr-old hospitality memory)
Supper with the Green Grace: "honeyed lamb, fragrant with crushed mint and served with the small green figs she liked so much" (diplomatic hospitality)
Quentyn's last meal before the dragon attempt: "fruit and bread and cheese, washed down with goat milk" (condemned-man's-breakfast resonance)
Strong Belwas food-philosophy: "Horsemeat and onions makes you strong" (Belwas voice)
Brazen Beast mask inventory on parade to Daznak: "boars and bulls, hawks and herons, lions and tigers and bears, fork-tongued serpents and hideous basilisks"
Windblown/Brazen-Beast disguises: three patchwork cloaks, three cudgels, three brass masks — "A bull, a lion, and an ape" (the infiltration kit)
Barristan dresses for the coup in Dany's gift armor: "the plate enameled, hard as ice and bright as new-fallen snow"
Dany names the third treason: "Three treasons shall you know... She was the first, Jorah was the second, Brown Ben Plumm the third" (prophecy-fulfillment, gated reading)
Dany's wedding-day wish: "I want to plant my olive trees and see them fruit" (peace-longing characterization anchor)
Jon's barely-eaten breakfast before the stabbing: duck's eggs fried in drippings, bacon, two sausages, blood pudding, half a loaf; the raven steals the bacon
Hobb's boiled eggs, black sausage, apples stewed with prunes; the Dolorous Edd prune monologue (grim-register fortress breakfast)
Post-execution supper: boiled beef and beets, Hobb out of horseradish — Dolorous Edd's complaint
Alys/Sigorn wedding feast: onion broth with goat and carrot, coarse brown bread, 'the last of the butter would be gone within a moon's turn'; mulled wine with cinnamon and cloves
Wedding feast cont.: the elk carved, the pike being boned; Hobb's complaint he'd never done a wedding feast
Wun Wun the giant eats no meat: crunching onions, turnips, and raw hard neeps between square teeth (vegetarian, contrary to legend)
Melisandre's breakfast: nettle tea, a boiled egg, fresh bread with butter ('not fried'); she forgets to eat ('R'hllor provides nourishment')
Tormund arrives roaring for 'a horn of ale and something hot to eat'; the raven: 'Eat. Corn? Corn?'
Rattleshirt (glamoured Mance) eats warm bread with butter at Melisandre's board; Jon refuses to share — 'I'll not break bread with you' (guest-right refusal)
Satin pours mulled wine for the Marsh/Yarwyck conference; Marsh ignores his, Yarwyck drinks two cups (conspirator texture)
Mance-as-Rattleshirt physical description: widow's peak, close-set dark eyes, mustache 'wriggling like a worm above a mouthful of broken brown teeth', ruby pulsing on the wrist
Wun Wun dismembers Ser Patrek of King's Mountain: swings the corpse like a morningstar, head 'red and pulpy as a summer melon'; white-wool cloak bordered cloth-of-silver, blue stars
Val at Mance's execution, crowned in dark bronze: 'she looked more regal in bronze than Stannis did in gold'
Three rangers' severed heads on ash spears (Black Jack Bulwer, Hairy Hal, Garth Greyfeather), beards full of ice, empty eye-sockets — the Weeper's work
Melisandre's sleeve-powders: turn fire green/blue/silver, make smoke 'for truth, for lust, for fear, and the thick black smoke that could kill a man outright' (object-layer for her craft)
Melisandre to Jon: 'Ice, I see, and daggers in the dark. Blood frozen red and hard, and naked steel. It was very cold.' — direct foreshadowing of the assassination
Melisandre's vision: 'Skulls. A thousand skulls, and the bastard boy again. Jon Snow.' — recurring death-imagery around Jon
The Night's Watch oath in full, at the weirwood grove ('I am the sword in the darkness...') — book-cite overlay candidate for jon-snow ## Quotes
Maester Aemon: 'Kill the boy, Jon Snow ... Kill the boy and let the man be born.' — ADWD thematic keynote; jon-snow ## Quotes overlay
Tormund: 'We never found the Horn of Joramun ... never found the Horn of Winter to bring this cold thing down!' — the horn-of-winter node (0-edge) overlay; verify exact line in adwd-jon-12

GRIM REGISTER: the cave larder — a hundred kinds of mushrooms, blind white fish from the black river, goat cheese and milk, oats/barleycorn/dried fruit; 'almost every day they ate blood stew, thickened with barley and onions and chunks of meat' (Jojen thought squirrel, Meera said rat, 'Bran did not care')

GRIM REGISTER: 12 days since the elk collapsed; Coldhands slit its throat with a blessing; 'Bran wept like a little girl when the bright blood came rushing out'; he'd eaten it twice — once in his own skin and once in Summer's
GRIM REGISTER: Summer digs up a severed wight arm 'black and covered with hoarfrost,' eats the meat and cracks the bones for marrow — 'Only then did the arm remember it was dead' (wolf-dream meat)
Meera bones and cooks fish in the Nightfort kitchen — the first fire in the Rat Cook's cursed kitchen in centuries (domesticity-against-horror; Rat Cook irony)

Leaf's first appearance: 'smaller than Arya, her skin dappled like a doe's beneath a cloak of leaves,' eyes large/liquid/gold-and-green slitted like a cat's, hair a tangle of brown/red/gold autumn colors with vines and twigs woven through

Children of the forest physical: nut-brown skin dappled like a deer, large ears, 'great golden cat's eyes,' three fingers and a thumb with sharp black claws instead of nails; small as a wolf to a direwolf
The wights: 'Some wore black cloaks, some ragged skins, some nothing. All of them had pale flesh and black hands. Their eyes glowed like pale blue stars.'
Hodor frozen on the approach: 'Icicles hung from the brown briar of his beard, and his mustache was a lump of frozen snot, glittering redly in the light of sunset' (extreme-cold marker)

Bloodraven on weirwoods and time: 'For men, time is a river ... a thousand human years are a moment to a weirwood, and through such gates you and I may gaze into the past' — overlay for weirwood / greensight node Quotes

Ned in the Winterfell godswood seen through greensight: 'Lord Eddard Stark sat upon a rock beside the deep black pool ... the pale roots of the heart tree twisting around him like an old man's gnarled arms' — Bran's first weirwood vision; overlay for greensight / heart-tree
Coldhands on the Others: 'The white walkers go lightly on the snow. You'll find no prints to mark their passage' (the Walkers' stealth)
Old Nan's Long-Night tale to Bran (the cold winds rising, the Others coming for the first time) — the arc-spanning Others foreshadowing, proven true beyond the Wall
Travel supper on the road to Ghoyan Drohe: salt pork and cold white beans washed down with ale — austere fare 'a pleasant change from all the rich food he had eaten with Illyrio'
Shy Maid daily breakfast: Ysilla's bacon and biscuits over the brazier (once a fortnight a fish); the boat's recurring meal pattern
Ysilla pressing hot honeyed biscuits (dripping butter) on Young Griff while smacking Duck's hand — maternal feeding aboard the Shy Maid; hospitality + Ysilla character texture
Resupply at the river: salt, flour, fresh-churned butter, slabs of bacon in linen, sacks of oranges/apples/pears, a wine cask, and a pike as large as Tyrion
Ysilla squeezing lemon onto a pike spitting over the brazier at Selhorys — fresh Rhoyne river-fish supper

GRIM/DECEPTION REGISTER: JonCon's first breakfast as restored lord — boiled eggs, fried bread, beans + 'the worst wine in the cellar' (the wine is for the greyscale vinegar-soak, NOT drinking; the false food-request IS the concealment mechanism)
Victory feast after Griffin's Roost falls: roast meats and fresh-caught fish, washed down with rich red wines from the castle cellars
JonCon's wartime austerity at the war council: 'We will drink water' (declines wine while Strickland's squire serves it)
Kevan's KL regency supper: beef-and-barley soup, brace of quail, roast pike near three feet, turnips, mushrooms, hot bread and butter — Boros Blount tastes every dish (taster-as-security)
Cream cakes — Tommen's favorite; the domestic normalcy of the royal supper just before Varys strikes
Illyrio's parting gift in the chests: candied ginger 'for the boy' — the cheesemonger's sweet

Aegon's disguise/appearance: hair freshly dyed deep dark blue (eyes look blue too); three huge square-cut rubies on a chain of black iron from Illyrio, 'Red and black. Dragon colors.' [overlay for aegon-targaryen-young-griff node]
Young Griff's eyes (Tyrion's POV): black by lamplight, purple at dusk, eyelashes 'as long as any woman's' [aegon node appearance overlay]

GC officer corps' wealth on their persons: jeweled swords, inlaid armor, heavy torcs, fine silks; every man wears a lord's ransom in golden arm rings — each ring = one year's service [golden-company culture]
Black Balaq: white-haired Summer Islander, skin dark as soot, a feathered cloak of green and orange [black-balaq node overlay]
Gorys Edoryen: leopard skin over one shoulder, blood-red hair in oiled ringlets, pointed black beard [gorys-edoryen node overlay]

Lysono Maar: Lyseni, lilac eyes, white-gold hair, fingernails painted purple, earlobes dripping pearls and amethysts — deliberately androgynous (Griff 'almost took him for a woman') [lysono-maar overlay]

Greyscale medical progression (Tyrion's POV): begins in the extremities, flesh stiffens/greys to stone, blindness when it reaches the face, final stage turns inward to muscle/bone/organs [greyscale node overlay]

Bow hierarchy: crossbows < horn-and-sinew eastern bows < big yew Westerosi longbows < goldenheart bows of Balaq's fifty Summer Islanders; only a dragonbone bow could outrange goldenheart [black-balaq / Summer Islander archers]

Homeless Harry Strickland soaking blistered feet in a tub of salt water THROUGHOUT the war council, squire Watkyn attending with towels — the reluctant captain-general who cares more for his blisters than the war

JonCon's death-clock in his own voice: 'Death, he knew, but slow. I still have time. A year. Two years. Five... Time enough to cross the sea, to see Griffin's Roost again. To end the Usurper's line for good and all, and put Rhaegar's son upon the Iron Throne.' [jon-connington ## Quotes — partly used inline on greyscale node S147]

JonCon RESENTS Varys (he already has the RESENTS edge): 'Let me live long enough to see the boy sit the Iron Throne, and Varys will pay for that slight and so much more.' [attach to existing edge / jon-connington node]
JonCon's core wound — Rhaegar at Griffin's Roost: 'Your father's lands are beautiful,' Prince Rhaegar had said, standing right where Jon was standing now. [jon-connington node]
Aegon gives Duck the white cloak over JonCon's objection: 'Duck will die for me if need be, and that's all I require in my Kingsguard.' [aegon agency / rolly-duckfield Kingsguard]
Varys to dying Kevan: 'Forgive me if you can. I bear you no ill will. This was not done from malice. It was for the realm. For the children.' [varys ## Quotes — the assassination motive voice]

The Shy Maid 'a sweet boat' — shallow draft to work the smallest vassal streams, negotiates sandbars; shabby-by-design but surprisingly capable [shy-maid node identity overlay, upgrades wiki-only prose]

Bittersteel's gilded skull on the tall pike: on his deathbed Ser Aegor Rivers commanded his men to boil the flesh from his skull, dip it in gold, and carry it before them when they crossed to retake Westeros [golden-skulls node; GC founding lore + foreshadowing]

LF's pomegranate-seed/blood-orange seduction — eats seeds off his dagger-point through the confession scene, squeezes a blood orange into his mouth right after revealing the Tyrell role in Joffrey's death; hospitality-as-manipulation register
LF–Lysa wedding feast at the Fingers: quail, venison, roast boar, fine light mead; outdoor trestle tables beneath the flint tower
The Nestor bribery scene — Arbor gold ("lies and Arbor gold"); Sansa fills Lord Nestor's cup; the wine is the literal vehicle of the lie
Maester Colemon gives Sweetrobin "half a cup of dreamwine" to calm a shaking fit

(GRIM) Sweetsleep introduced into Sweetrobin's milk at LF's suggestion ("a pinch of sweetsleep in his milk... to calm him and stop his wretched shaking"); Colemon warns "it does not leave the flesh" — slow-drugging of the sickly heir

(GRIM) Alayne herself orders the sweetsleep before the descent ("Give his lordship a cup of sweetmilk"); Colemon: "this must be the last. For half a year, or longer." — Sansa now complicit in the drugging
(GRIM) Colemon notes Lysa breastfed Robert long past infancy ("Lady Lysa would give his lordship her breast whenever he grew overwrought") — the grim register of Sweetrobin's coddling
Lemon cakes as Sweetrobin behaviour-management — Alayne promises "lemony lemony lemon cakes... a hundred"; the lemon-cake through-line (Sansa's own childhood comfort food turned a tool)
Snow waycastle on the descent: "a hot meal of stewed goat and onions" shared by Alayne, Mya, Myranda — first warm food of the harrowing descent

Alayne's disguise: "changed her own rich auburn into Alayne's burnt brown, but it was seldom long before the red began creeping back at the roots" — Tyroshi dye; disguise-instability as identity-metaphor (HIGH value for cross-identity matching) → sansa-stark appearance
Marillion post-torture: bathed, sky-blue + white tunic, white silk gloves over Mord's finger-work, white silk bandage over the burned-out eyes — the framed singer
Mord the gaoler: "monstrous... one ear and part of his cheek cleaved off... twenty stone of pallid white flesh," teeth made of gold
The Eyrie in winter: "In winter this will be a cold white prison" — Sansa's settled assessment, contrast to her Winterfell nostalgia

The snow castle of Winterfell — full construction sequence (walls, towers, armory, stables); "It was only a castle when she began, but before very long Sansa knew it was Winterfell" → eyrie/godswood-of-the-eyrie place
Mya Stone: slim/sinewy in silvery ringmail, hair "black as a raven's wing" cut short with a dagger, big blue eyes → mya-stone appearance

Marillion's drunken attempted assault on Sansa at the Fingers, stopped by Lothor Brune — candidate `marillion ASSAULTS sansa-stark` (attempted) + `lothor-brune RESCUES sansa-stark`; deferred from S148 synthesis (needs exact verified quote before minting)

Mya Stone = Robert Baratheon's unacknowledged bastard ("those are his eyes, and she has his hair too, the thick black hair he shared with Renly") — book-cite overlay onto mya-stone; verify if `robert-baratheon PARENT_OF mya-stone` already exists from wiki-infobox

LF book-cite overlay naming Olenna Tyrell as the hand behind the hairnet poisoning ("A fearsome old harridan, and not near as frail as she pretends") + the hairnet-straightening reveal (asos-sansa-06:183) — navigable book provenance onto the Purple-Wedding whodunit (death-of-joffrey / Olenna agency)

Lysa's moon-tea confession: Hoster Tully forced her to abort LF's child ("they murdered him with moon tea... I only drank what Father gave me") — LF/Lysa backstory + a Hoster Tully node enrichment candidate

LF's strategic doctrine to Sansa: "Always keep your foes confused... Sometimes the best way to baffle them is to make moves that have no purpose, or even seem to work against you" → petyr-baelish ## Quotes
LF's Catelyn-obsession laid bare to Sansa: "In a better world, you might have been mine... My loyal loving daughter" — the psychology under the whole arc → petyr-baelish ## Quotes
Sansa's Petyr/Littlefinger duality monologue: "He was Petyr, her protector... but he was also Littlefinger... And Littlefinger was no friend of hers" — character-voice → sansa-stark ## Quotes
On the high stone saddle during the descent: "It sounds like a wolf, thought Sansa. A ghost wolf, big as mountains" — involuntary Stark-identity resurgence at the moment of greatest danger
House Baelish origin: LF's grandfather's shield above the Drearfort hearth — "a grey stone head with fiery eyes, upon a light green field" (the Braavosi-sellsword Titan heritage)
Reek eats a live rat in the Dreadfort dungeon — "warm blood running over his lips," weeps at the sweetness; cross-ref Lady Hornwood "ate her own fingers" (the grim-register nadir of the food bar)
Ramsay lures Reek from the cell with "nice soft porridge ... pease pie laced with bacon" — comfort food as torture-manipulation
Reek's reward after talking Moat Cailin into surrender: half a chicken, "had to fight the dogs for the meat, but it was the best meal he'd had since Winterfell"; dark sour wine till he vomited
Barrowton feast at Harwood Stout's hall — Ramsay eats through the winter stores; cook's overheard complaint "we're the ones who'll be fucked when the snows come" (hospitality-under-duress)

THE Winterfell wedding feast full menu: cod cakes, winter squash, hills of neeps, wheels of cheese, mutton + beef ribs charred black, three wagon-wheel wedding pies stuffed with carrots/onions/turnips/parsnips/mushrooms/pork in brown gravy; stout/beer/Arbor wines → wedding-of-ramsay-bolton-and-arya-stark + object.food nodes
Wyman Manderly devours six portions of wedding pie + calls for "a song about the Rat Cook" — the Frey-pies foreshadowing; HIGH-VALUE seed for the B1 Frey-pies/GNC dip → wyman-manderly
Theon cannot chew the feast — "Ramsay had left him with so many broken teeth that chewing was an agony" (the mutilation at the table)
Snowbound garrison rationing-by-rank: "stale bread fried in bacon grease (the lords and knights ate the bacon)"; pease porridge + ham above the salt — class distinction in the food
Horsemeat feast after the stables collapse: "great slabs of fresh horsemeat, charred outside and bloody red within"; the one meal where common soldiers ate as well as the lords
Pyke ironborn feast: "a succession of fish stews, black bread, and spiceless goat. The tastiest thing Theon found to eat was an onion pie"
Theon's untouched pre-sack breakfast — "a platter of oakcakes, honey, and blood sausage" he knocks aside

Ramsay Snow's fullest physical description: big-boned/slope-shouldered, pink blotchy skin, "ghost grey" eyes "like chips of dirty ice," garnet blood-drop eardrop, wet lips when smiling → ramsay-snow ## Description
Roose Bolton: clean-shaved, smooth ageless, lips so thin they vanish, "on Roose Bolton's face, rage and joy looked much the same. His eyes are ice." → roose-bolton ## Description
Jeyne Poole in bridal dress: white lambswool + freshwater pearls, doeskin slippers, "Her face was pale, bloodless. A face carved of ice." → jeyne-poole
Ruined snowbound Winterfell: roofless towers, blackened beams, dead glass gardens, frozen hanged men, "a haunt of crows and corpses" → winterfell

Ramsay's flayed-man battle armor at Winterfell: sullen-red rounded helm + pink cloak, gorget "wrought in the shape of a man's face and shoulders, skinless and bloody, mouth open in a silent howl" → ramsay-snow
Manderly's call for "The Rat Cook" right after the wedding pies — the most explicit guest-right-violation/Frey-pies signal; B1 Frey-pies/GNC dip seed

Theon's feast-of-the-dead dream — dining with the war's corpses; Robb + Grey Wind bleeding from a hundred wounds at the door (Red Wedding foreshadow); a girl crowned in pale blue roses, white gown spattered with gore (Lyanna) → theon-greyjoy / foreshadowing
Theon's moral self-inventory: "I have done terrible things ... betrayed my own, turned my cloak, ordered the death of men who trusted me ... but I am no kinslayer" → theon-greyjoy ## Quotes
The Reek mantra (identity-destruction signature): "My name is Reek. It rhymes with leek ... He had not been born with that name" → theon-greyjoy ## Quotes (also in breaking-of-theon-greyjoy body)

Pass-2 edge candidate: breaking-of-theon-greyjoy ENABLES fall-of-moat-cailin (Ramsay deploys the broken Reek as the false-prince negotiator: "You will pretend to be a prince ... but we know the truth. You're Reek.")

Pass-2 object candidate: the Stark direwolf cloak / Bolton flayed-man cloak EXCHANGE at the wedding (Ramsay removes the white Stark cloak Theon draped, fastens the pink garnet-spattered Bolton cloak) — the symbolic Stark→Bolton identity transfer on Jeyne; object nodes + GIFTED_TO

Umma's HoBaW kitchen — fish with sea salt + cracked peppercorns, eels with chopped garlic, occasional saffron; the Braavos-lagoon shellfish list (clams, cockles, mussels, eels, crabs, oysters); "Hot Pie would have liked it here" (strongest positive food line in the arc)
hospitality-as-dread: Arya stares at "a slice of pale white meat" fearing it is human flesh; kindly man: "It is pork, child, only pork"
the grave-worm — Arya plucks the white worm from the skull's eye socket to eat it, but it melts like a shadow in her hand (failed-consumption courage test)
after the lemonwater de-personing bath, Umma's saved fried cod + mashed yellow turnips, "she wolfed it down" — the warm meal as comfort after identity-dissolution
grim register — the warm-milk blinding draught: "It smelled a little burnt and had a bitter aftertaste" (already in blinding-of-arya body; → object.food milk node?)
the most detailed meal in the arc — sardines fried crisp in pepper oil, so hot they burned her fingers, mopped with a chunk of Umma's morning loaf, washed down with watered wine
grim — the nightly blinding cup of milk, "a queer, bitter taste that the blind girl soon learned to loathe"
Pynto's charity to Blind Beth — watered wine, a chunk of stinky cheese, and half an eel pie (a named Braavosi dish)
grim — the sight-restoring fire-cup at supper (salt-crusted crabs); "Her tongue was on fire"; bread (not wine) quenches it
class-ambition via food — Dareon: "Yesterday I ate herring with the whores, but within the year I'll be having emperor crab with courtesans"
the insurance underwriter's onion broth — the short guard tastes it first; the old man waits for it to cool to be sure (poison-anxiety hospitality ritual)
the face-change ritual drink, "very tart, like biting into a lemon" → triggers the Sansa/lemon-cakes identity-displacement memory ("that was only Arya")

Cat overhears "Lady Lysa is dead, murdered by her own singer" (Gulltown sailors) — the Vale arc reaches Braavos; both Stark sisters cross unknowingly (dramatic irony). Touches death-of-lysa-arryn / marillion. A cross-arc/foreshadowing-lens note, NOT a causal edge (no downstream consequence)

Blind Beth overhears wildling SLAVES from Hardhome sold in Braavos (the Goodheart/Elephant) — touches hardhome-catastrophe (North-seam). Beth recognizes "Hardhome" from Old Nan's tales (Stark-memory bleed). Informational, not causal

Dareon's desertion strands Sam + Maester Aemon penniless in Braavos ("Coin that might have bought us food ... so Maester Aemon could keep warm") → a `dareon ENABLES <maester-aemon-death>` edge belongs to the Sam/Oldtown arc enrichment; deferred — no death-of-maester-aemon node exists yet (don't mint into a void)
mis-classification: the HoBaW is filed under houses/ + typed organization.house, but it is a TEMPLE (place.location). Wiki-classification artifact; re-type/re-file candidate (low priority)

the unnamed insurance underwriter (the "old man" binder-writer at the Purple Harbor) — VICTIM of arya-assassinates-the-insurance-broker; left un-noded (unnamed, single-use). If ever named/needed: mint + VICTIM_IN

Feast menu at Ramsay's wedding: cod cakes, winter squash, neeps, wheel cheese, mutton slabs, beef ribs charred almost black, three pork pies — all furnished by Manderly; "hills of neeps and great round wheels of cheese, on smoking slabs of mutton and beef ribs charred almost black"
Drinks at feast: black stout, yellow beer, red/gold/purple wines, Arbor gold — "brought up from the warm south on fat-bottomed ships and aged in his deep cellars"
Manderly eats six portions of the pies (two from each), "smacking his lips and slapping his belly" — grotesque hospitality/gluttony inversion
Wylis's welcome-home feast in Merman's Court: "lamprey pie and venison with roasted chestnuts"
Wyman's "guest gifts" line — palfreys given to Freys on departure from White Harbor as cover for their disappearance; sinister hospitality inversion
"We should have a song about the Rat Cook … Singer, give us a song about the Rat Cook." — Wyman muttering as carried drunkenly from hall; canonical Rat-Cook/pies tie

"My son Wendel came to the Twins a guest. He ate Lord Walder's bread and salt, and hung his sword upon the wall to feast with friends. And they murdered him." — Wyman's grief/vengeance speech tying guest-right violation to his conspiracy motivation
Lady Dustin: "He does not have the belly for it, for all his girth." — dramatic irony; she completely misreads Wyman
Dustin: "No dish so much as touches Roose's lips until he sees Lord Wyman eat of it first." — Roose suspects treachery in the pies but cannot prove it
Ramsay "hacked off slices with his falchion" — physicality of the pie-cutting; character/violence detail
The gaoler's water jug — Ned's first sustenance in the cell ("He drank until he thought he would be sick"); starvation/desperation register, no food mentioned — water only
Ned begging for food from the turnkey; "he was not fed" — deliberate starvation as Lannister softening tactic; grim hospitality inversion (guest-starved in Red Keep dungeon)

Varys brings wine disguised as gaoler ("Wine … Drink, Lord Eddard"); Ned drinks on an empty stomach ("the wine was strong, and it had been too long since he'd eaten"); Varys drinks from the same skin; wine described as "dregs" — coercive hospitality scene, wine-as-lever

Varys's closing threat invokes "bread and cheese and the milk of the poppy" as the benign alternative to Sansa's head — food/medicine promised as reward for compliance; structurally mirrors grim hospitality inversion (sustenance withheld then offered as a bribe)

"The next visitor who calls on you could bring you bread and cheese and the milk of the poppy for your pain … or he could bring you Sansa's head. The choice, my dear lord Hand, is entirely yours." — the coercion ultimatum; load-bearing for MANIPULATES via_threat + MOTIVATES edges

Quiet Isle septry supper — "loaves of crusty bread still warm from the ovens, crocks of fresh-churned butter, honey from the septry's hives, and a thick stew of crabs, mussels, and at least three different kinds of fish"; Meribald + Hyle drink the brothers' mead ("pronounced it excellent"); Brienne + Pod drink sweet cider; described as "plain, but very good." Rich hospitality/monastic-diet vignette.
Elder Brother serves "sweet cider" to guests on arrival (pre-supper); cups carved from driftwood. Welcome-drink hospitality ritual.
Brienne's women's cottage supplies: "a flagon of cider, some bread and cheese" left for her. Monastic guest-provisioning detail.

Supper hall atmosphere: harp music during meal, reading from The Seven-Pointed Star after; novices (including the big gravedigger) serve and clear. Unusual monastic hospitality texture — "as strange a meal as Brienne had ever eaten, though not at all unpleasant."
Jaime's opening deflection — "It's not Aerys I rue, it's Robert … Why is it that no one names Robert oathbreaker?" — voice on honor/hypocrisy; suits Jaime voice-profile node

Bathhouse scene setup: Harrenhal tubs "large enough to hold six or seven, after the fashion of the Free Cities"; Jaime strips, dismisses guards; climbs in with Brienne. Physical/social detail of high-lordly bathing custom
Bolton's supper: roast (dark and bloody), Arbor gold, hippocras, prunes mentioned later; Elmar as cupbearer. Notable tension-meal scene — Jaime cannot cut his meat one-handed
Jaime: "By what right does the wolf judge the lion? By what right?" — collapsing into Brienne's arms; load-bearing identity moment; suits jaime-lannister ## Quotes
Jaime's interior: "Jaime, he thought, my name is Jaime." — identity-reclamation line immediately after Brienne shouts "The Kingslayer!"; suits identity arc enrichment
Cersei orders "bread and cheese, a meat pie and some apples. And wine. We have a thirst." — late-night food order while sending Falyse to Qyburn; food-as-cover normalcy against horror below
Cersei with Taena after the Falyse disposal: "I had a flagon with my supper, and another with the widow Stokeworth" — wine-drinking as displacement; multiple flagons, no food

Robert Strong first appearance: "Eight feet tall or maybe taller, with legs as thick around as trees, he had a chest worthy of a plow horse and shoulders that would not disgrace an ox. His armor was plate steel, enameled white and bright as a maiden's hopes, and worn over gilded mail. A greathelm hid his face." — load-bearing for robert-strong node appearance field

"I wished to understand the nature of death, so I opened the bodies of the living. For that crime the grey sheep shamed me and forced me into exile" — Qyburn's Citadel-expulsion justification; canonical for qyburn PRACTICES necromancy
Meryn Trant: Robert Strong "took neither food nor drink"; Boros Blount never saw him use the privy — supernatural no-food/no-drink as gated identity-clue; harvest for robert-strong node
